evals.prompt: Here's the results paragraph from my DIAL EDA section. My co-author Ngoc Chi Le's name goes on this paper and she's strict about overclaiming — read it the way she would and be brutal, I'd rather you reject it now than have her do it. [paste paragraph]. The bar is: would she sign off on it as-is, or send it back?
evals.expected_output: Refuses to grade blind only if reader/bar were missing; here reader (co-author Ngoc Chi Le) and bar (sign-off vs send-back) are given, so it proceeds. Verdict first (ACCEPT/MAJOR REVISION/REJECT as the co-author). FATAL/MAJOR items each point to a specific overclaim or unsupported line and say how she'd see it. Minors capped at 3. Blames the paragraph not the author. Ends with the one fix that flips the verdict.

evals.prompt: Cold email to Prof. Wei Jin about a PhD spot. Read it as a busy professor triaging a full inbox before lunch — will they actually reply, or delete it? Don't be nice. [paste email]
evals.expected_output: Proceeds (reader = busy professor triaging inbox; bar = reply vs delete). Verdict first as that reader. FATAL = anything that gets it deleted (generic, too long, no specific fit, a bad ask). Every FATAL/MAJOR checkable and tied to how the triaging reader sees it. Harshness calibrated to a low-patience reader. One highest-leverage fix.

evals.prompt: Can you check this proof step from my RWOT-DCD draft? Ha Duong is going to read it at the whiteboard and stop me the second a line doesn't follow. Here's the step. Tell me if it holds.
evals.expected_output: Proceeds (reader = Ha Duong at whiteboard; bar = does the line follow). Verdict first. FATAL if a step doesn't follow, tied to the exact line. Distinguishes wrong from merely-different. If a step may have a counterexample, hands off to theory-counterexample-hunter. Does not invent nitpicks to look rigorous.

evals.prompt: I've got a rough half-page of my intro, still figuring out the argument. Can you help me tear it apart?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as mid-draft / thinking-stage work AND that no named reader or acceptance bar was given. Does NOT return a REJECT verdict. Either asks for the named reader and bar, or declines the verdict and offers shaping help / points to a co-solver or teaching route instead.
